headline: China’s Antimony Ban Sent Prices Up 2,600%. Rare Earths Are Next
summary_raw: In 2024, China announced export controls on antimony.      It’s a metal most people have never heard of, but antimony goes into more than 200 types of military munitions. Within weeks, the price went from $1,400 per ton to $38,000 — a 2,600% spike — and shipments to the United States fell 97%. Today, Beijing is setting up for something bigger. headline: PRA sets out adjustments to its market risk internal model approach under Basel 3.1
